channel.title: South Asia – The Diplomat
channel.description: The Diplomat is a current-affairs magazine for the Asia-Pacific, with news and analysis on politics, security, business, technology and life across the region.

item.title: Modi’s Saudi Arabia Visit Sets Tone For Long-term Engagement
item.description: The joint statement was unequivocal in condemning the Pahalgam attack. And while it did not name Pakistan, it condemned “cross-border terrorism.”
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Roche

item.title: After Pahalgam, India Faces Tough Security and Diplomatic Choices
item.description: Previously, militants targeted security personnel through ambush-style assaults or hit-and-run gun attacks on the Pandit community. The attack in Pahalgam was aimed at tourists.
item.dc:creator: Aishwaria Sonavane

item.title: Why the Balochistan Mines and Minerals Act Was Enacted Swiftly and Silently
item.description: The controversial law will undermine restive Balochistan’s rights over its mineral reserves.
item.dc:creator: Mariyam Suleman Anees

item.title: Lee Jae-myung Elected as DP’s Presidential Candidate
item.description: After setting a record for the share of votes received during the party’s primary elections, Lee aims to win the presidential election scheduled on June 3.
item.dc:creator: Mitch Shin

item.title: Nepal’s Dilemma Over Social Media Regulation
item.description: Nepal is facing a fast-escalating digital crisis that its current legal, technical, and institutional systems are not yet equipped to handle.
item.dc:creator: Shree Gurung

item.title: India’s Indus Ultimatum: South Asia’s Red Line Moment
item.description: The tit-for-tat measures announced by India and Pakistan resemble a classic prisoner’s dilemma, where each side views escalatory retaliation as rational -- even necessary -- despite mutual harm.
item.dc:creator: Mirza M. Hamza

item.title: What Causes Hindu Emigration From Pakistan’s Sindh Province?
item.description: Due to the deteriorating security situation and the overall economic conditions in Pakistan, many Hindus feel compelled to migrate.
item.dc:creator: Muhammad Murad

item.title: Why Bangladesh’s Muhammad Yunus Must Hold Elections Soon
item.description: A democratically elected political government would have more legitimacy than the interim administration to move the country forward.
item.dc:creator: Mubashar Hasan

item.title: Pahalgam: Why Would Pakistan Risk an Attack Now?
item.description: A war with India is one of the few things that can unify Pakistanis and distract from its numerous problems.
item.dc:creator: Akhilesh Pillalamarri

item.title: Terror Attack Shatters Kashmir’s Myth of Peace: Voices From the Valley
item.description: New Delhi believed its own narrative – that peace had returned to the Kashmir Valley. That myth was shattered on April 22.
item.dc:creator: Shanthie Mariet D’Souza

item.title: India’s Suspension of the Indus Waters Treaty With Pakistan Could Backfire
item.description: It could prompt China to block the Brahmaputra River, which supplies around 30 percent of India’s fresh water and about 44 percent of its total hydropower potential.
item.dc:creator: Umair Jamal

item.title: Despite Modi’s Claims of Peace, Kashmir Burns
item.description: Even prior to the deadly attack on April 22, Jammu and Kashmir had seen an uptick in violence – despite the government’s insistence that peace prevailed.
item.dc:creator: Tarushi Aswani

item.title: The Indus Waters Treaty: Colonial Legacies, Cold War Geopolitics, and Climate Challenges
item.description: For India, the treaty's suspension offers a chance for renegotiation, while Pakistan faces limited options to uphold its current status.
item.dc:creator: Atal Ahmadzai

item.title: Inequalities of Caste and Contract in Sewage Work in Delhi
item.description: Rising contractual employment in sanitation work draws upon and reproduces historical inequalities of caste and class in urban India.
item.dc:creator: Aarushie Sharma

item.title: Why Sri Lanka’s Rejection of a Land Bridge With India Is the Right Call
item.description: Physical connection to India could dissuade other countries from deepening ties with the island, as it will be seen as New Delhi’s client state.
item.dc:creator: Rathindra Kuruwita

item.title: Plummeting Academic Freedom and Autonomy in Modi’s India
item.description: India figures in the bottom 15 percent of 179 countries with regard to academic freedom, according to V-Dem’s 2025 report.
item.dc:creator: Kavita Chowdhury

item.title: Terrorists Target Tourist Hotspot in India’s Jammu and Kashmir
item.description: The Resistance Group, an affiliate of the Pakistan-based Lashkar-e-Taiba, has claimed responsibility for the mass killings. How will India respond?
item.dc:creator: Sudha Ramachandran

item.title: The Unfolding Sorry Saga of Nepal’s China-built Airport at Pokhara
item.description: A parliamentary panel probing the project has discovered irregularities worth over $100 million.
item.dc:creator: Biswas Baral

item.title: The Dragon-Elephant Dance: China and India’s Battle for South Asia
item.description: Yun Sun and Jagannath Panda discuss China and India's battle for influence and power projection across South Asia.
item.dc:creator: Tushar Shetty

item.title: Starlink in Bangladesh: A Digital Leap or Sovereignty Trade-Off?
item.description: While Starlink holds promise for connecting Bangladesh’s rural and hard-to-reach areas, it also raises critical questions about national autonomy, data ownership, and digital governance.
item.dc:creator: Shahadat Swadhin

item.title: India, France to Sign Contract for 26 Rafale Marine Fighters
item.description: At $7.6 billion, this is the Indian military’s costliest defense purchase ever.
item.dc:creator: Ajai Shukla

item.title: Why Are Tokay Geckos Being Smuggled Out of India’s Northeast?
item.description: This lizard species, which is valued for its use in traditional medicine in China and Southeast Asia, can earn a smuggler around $70,000 per lizard.
item.dc:creator: Rajeev Bhattacharyya

item.title: Bhutanese Refugees Deported From the US Find Themselves Stateless Once More
item.description: A former refugee, legally resettled in America after exile from Bhutan 30 years ago, has been sent back decades later as Trump’s anti-immigration agenda revives old horrors.
item.dc:creator: Nishchal Aawaz

item.title: Balochistan’s War on Journalism: When Truth-telling Becomes a Death Sentence
item.description: Balochistan has long been the most deadly place in an already dangerous country for journalists. Here, speaking truth to power isn’t just risky; it is often fatal.
item.dc:creator: Dilshad Baluch

item.title: The Emerging Exception: India Sees Itself as Transcending Middle Power
item.description: India no longer sees itself as merely a middle power, but as a key player shaping the global order: an “emerging great power.”
item.dc:creator: Rajiv Bhatia

item.title: The National Security Implications of Starlink’s Entry Into India
item.description: India’s uncritical and pernicious reliance on U.S. tech companies may serve to undermine its own national and technological visions.
item.dc:creator: Gurshabad Grover

item.title: The Evolution of the Baloch Liberation Army
item.description: With growing operational capacity, BLA is targeting Chinese projects, blocking highways, and briefly taking over control of remote towns.
item.dc:creator: Abdul Basit

item.title: Bangladesh Pokes India in the Eye; Delhi Hits Back
item.description: India’s withdrawal of transshipment facilities for Bangladeshi cargo headed for third countries will hit the Bangladeshi economy hard.
item.dc:creator: Sudha Ramachandran

item.title: Through Climate Diplomacy, Nepal Sounds the Alarm on the Himalayas
item.description: Nepal’s climate vulnerability shapes its climate goals and strategies, with the Sagarmatha Sambad as a rising symbol of Himalayan climate diplomacy.
item.dc:creator: Ritesh Panthee

item.title: Water for Peace: What Bangladesh Wants From the Ganga Water Treaty
item.description: The forthcoming negotiations for renewing the treaty must make water-sharing climate resilient and equitable as well as account for extreme weather conditions.
item.dc:creator: Sk Tawfique M Haque

item.title: Critical Minerals Add a New Dynamic to Pakistan-US Relations
item.description: However, Pakistan will need to improve security in Balochistan, where these resources are located.
item.dc:creator: Umair Jamal

item.title: Hindutva’s Food Allergy: Vegetarian Notion of Their ‘Hindu Nation’
item.description: Although Indians are predominantly meat eaters, Hindutva activists are enforcing a fanatic vegetarianism on everyone, irrespective of their beliefs, food, and cultural habits.
item.dc:creator: Snigdhendu Bhattacharya

item.title: India’s Coal Phasedown Needs a Balanced Socio-economic Framework
item.description: A people-centric transition of coal mine workers needs timely reskilling, training, relocation and rehabilitation as part of India's transition to clean energy.
item.dc:creator: Anvesha Adhikari, Navya, Anjali Goyal, Anandajit Goswami and Vaibhav Chowdhary

item.title: We Owe Afghan Women a Chance to Make Their Own Destiny
item.description: “We urge the United Nations to bring Afghan women to the negotiating table, to be their own advocates and create their own destiny, before it is too late.”
item.dc:creator: Nazila Jamshidi and Annie Pforzheimer

item.title: Reimagining the Bangladesh-China Strategic Partnership
item.description: As Bangladesh redefines its foreign policy, its ability to balance regional and global interests will determine the future trajectory of Bangladesh-China relations in a rapidly evolving geopolitical landscape.
item.dc:creator: Shafi Md Mostofa and Tamim Muntasir

item.title: Asanga Abeyagoonasekera on the India-Sri Lanka Defense Cooperation Agreement
item.description: “The DCA marks a significant strategic shift, drawing Sri Lanka more firmly into India’s regional security calculus.”
item.dc:creator: Sudha Ramachandran

item.title: The USAID Freeze: A View From Sri Lanka and the Global South
item.description: Donald Trump’s gutting of the American aid agency has adversely impacted humanitarian work in countries like Sri Lanka – while calling into question the reliability of Western foreign aid in the Global South.
item.dc:creator: Uditha Devapriya and Omar Rajarathnam

item.title: A Closer Look at India’s Global Influence on Human Rights
item.description: India remains a vibrant democracy. Yet its senior leaders remain fundamentally illiberal, and their interests more closely align with authoritarian powers.
item.dc:creator: Ria Chakrabarty

item.title: After FM Phone Call, Are Pakistan-US Ties on the Mend?
item.description: The first call between Ishaq Dar and Marco Rubio was another encouraging sign, but great uncertainty remains in the bilateral relationship.
item.dc:creator: Muhammad Murad

item.title: Minilateralism Is the Driver of India’s Diplomatic Strategy in the Indo-Pacific
item.description: As question marks are raised globally about the future of multilateralism, can India’s approach meet its strategic goals in the Indo-Pacific?
item.dc:creator: Ambika Vishwanath

item.title: Can a Leadership Change Revive the Indian Left?
item.description: 71-year-old Mariam Alexander Baby has taken charge of the CPI(M), India’s largest leftist party, amid its worst organizational crisis.
item.dc:creator: Snigdhendu Bhattacharya

item.title: Bangladesh Offers to Import More From the US
item.description: The country’s interim adviser, Muhammad Yunus, a noted economist, has responded swiftly to Trump’s tariffs with a contingency plan.
item.dc:creator: Abu Jakir

item.title: India Launches Multinational Mission of Cooperation in Indian Ocean Region
item.description: IOS Sagar is bringing together multiple maritime agencies of the Southwest Indian Ocean Region for joint training on an Indian naval platform.
item.dc:creator: Ajai Shukla

item.title: The Case for the US and Bhutan to Establish Formal Diplomatic Relations
item.description: Conditions are ripening to establish official Bhutan-U.S. ties, a goal supported by three trends: tourism, high-level communication, and the strengthening India-U.S. relationship.
item.dc:creator: Abhinav Seetharaman

item.title: How India Should Respond to Trump’s Tariff Threat
item.description: India should take this opportunity to drop tariffs on US manufacturers to zero – for reasons that go far beyond Trump's “Liberation Day.”
item.dc:creator: Mohsin Raza Khan

item.title: The India-US Nuclear Deal: Untying the Gordian Knot of Nuclear Liability
item.description: Amending India’s nuclear liability law could be the key to fully reviving the deal – and the Indian nuclear energy market.
item.dc:creator: Sanaa Alvira

item.title: American Leaders Should Hold the Taliban Accountable, Not Engage With Them
item.description: It’s past time for the U.S. and the international community to use the tools at their disposal – such as expanding targeted sanctions – to hold the Taliban to account.
item.dc:creator: Natalie Gonnella-Platts

item.title: The BM-04 Missile: India’s Next-Generation Conventional Counterforce Weapon
item.description: The BM-04 missile development confirms a military doctrine aimed at conducting limited operations under the nuclear umbrella at conventional and subconventional levels.
item.dc:creator: Usman Haider

item.title: Mahrang Baloch Takes on the Might of the Pakistani State
item.description: The peaceful resistance she leads is hamstrung by limited financial resources and global networking opportunities to mount an international campaign.
item.dc:creator: Sanjay Pulipaka

item.title: From Revolution to Reform: The BNP’s Uphill Battle in a New Bangladesh
item.description: Despite its historical popularity, the party is losing ground due to a combination of internal weaknesses, external pressures, and shifting public expectations.
item.dc:creator: Shafi Md Mostofa

item.title: Unlocking India-South Korea Trade Opportunities: Insights from the Recent Partnership Economic Forum in Seoul
item.description: Amid Trump’s tariffs, the economic partnership between India and South Korea becomes more critical for both sides.
item.dc:creator: Rachit Goel

item.title: Asia Holds a Few Trump Cards in the Global Trade War
item.description: Trump started the trade war, but Asia’s response is what matters.
item.dc:creator: Chayu Damsinghe

item.title: Modi’s India Is a Dangerous Place for Standup Comedians
item.description: Right-wing political activists went on a rampage against comedian Kunal Kamra for cracking jokes about ruling party politicians.
item.dc:creator: Kavita Chowdhury

item.title: Reimagining India’s Blue Water Navy: Toward a Comprehensive Maritime Doctrine for the Indian Ocean
item.description: A truly Indianized maritime doctrine, backed by comprehensive policy instruments, is not only necessary – it is inevitable.
item.dc:creator: Kunal Sharma

item.title: Pakistan Sees Opportunity in Trump’s Tariffs
item.description: Its main Asian competitors for access to the U.S. textile market have been slapped with higher tariffs.
item.dc:creator: Umair Jamal

item.title: India Tightens Grip Over Sri Lanka With Defense Pact
item.description: Sri Lanka’s JVP-led NPP government has made a dramatic pivot toward India, even utilizing legislative changes it once vehemently opposed.
item.dc:creator: Rathindra Kuruwita

item.title: ‘Not Just Drowning’: Gujjar Community Demands Justice Over Suspicious Deaths in Kashmir
item.description: Police say the men drowned accidentally, but families and activists allege torture and demand an independent probe into mysterious deaths in Kashmir.
item.dc:creator: Sajad Hameed and Rehan Qayoom

item.title: The Message and Consequences of Removing Haqqani Network Leaders From the US ‘Rewards for Justice’ List
item.description: History has shown us that giving in to extremist actors does not bring about justice or achieve peace.
item.dc:creator: Besmillah Taban

item.title: Growing Closeness Between Russia and the Taliban: A Paradigm Shift?
item.description: If the Taliban are removed from Moscow's terrorist list, it would further strengthen the relationship between Russia and Afghanistan.
item.dc:creator: Muhammad Murad

item.title: ‘Right Time For India to Intervene Aggressively In Myanmar’: NUG
item.description: The appeal came days before the Indian prime minister met junta leader Gen Min Aung Hlaing in Bangkok.
item.dc:creator: Rajeev Bhattacharyya

item.title: India and Canada Eye Resetting Relations
item.description: A rapprochement will not be easy or swift. Canadian public support for an FTA with India is low.
item.dc:creator: Rushali Saha

item.title: Asian Migrant Women Are Trapped in Lebanon’s Abusive ‘Kafala’ System
item.description: Regional conflict has intensified the vulnerabilities of Asian domestic migrant workers, leaving many without legal safeguards, financial support, or options to return home.
item.dc:creator: Nicholas Muller

item.title: BIMSTEC: Missing in Action in Myanmar
item.description: The grouping has emphasized disaster response in its activities, yet its response to Myanmar’s catastrophic earthquake is sorely lacking.
item.dc:creator: Angshuman Choudhury

item.title: At BIMSTEC’s Bangkok Summit, Leaders Pledge Economic Integration Amid Global Flux
item.description: With India shifting focus away from SAARC to BIMSTEC, it is keen to see the latter succeed.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Roche

item.title: Nepali PM Oli Meets India’s Modi, But Has Little to Show For It
item.description: Despite the Bangkok meeting, Oli has not gotten an invitation to visit New Delhi, something which he is desperately trying to secure.
item.dc:creator: Biswas Baral

item.title: New Law Gives Government a Foothold in Indian Muslim Waqf Properties
item.description: Opposition parties and Muslim groups are challenging the legislation’s constitutional validity in the Supreme Court.
item.dc:creator: Sudha Ramachandran

item.title: What Trump’s New Tariff Rules Mean for South Asia
item.description: Uncertainty looms. But since the tariffs are also squeezing global competitors, South Asian countries could, with the right policies, recover.
item.dc:creator: Saqlain Rizve

item.title: Tariffs and Inflation for the US, Disruption for Indian Supply Chains
item.description: India must work at making India great as opposed to bending over backwards to help Trump in Making America Great Again.
item.dc:creator: Anuradha Chenoy

item.title: The India-US Trade Dilemma: Tariffs, Tensions, and the Road Ahead
item.description: New Delhi faces a difficult choice between attempting to ease tariffs through diplomatic channels or imposing counter-tariffs on U.S. goods.
item.dc:creator: Kashif Hasan Khan

item.title: Modi’s Waqf Bill a Potential Death Blow for Muslim Property Rights
item.description: Although the Indian government has justified the revisions in terms of transparency and anti-corruption, there is little reason to assume it is acting in good faith.
item.dc:creator: Rasheed Ahmed

item.title: The Man Who Would be King Again? Former Nepal King’s Delusions of Power
item.description: The rise of Hindu fundamentalist and monarchist groups has made Nepal volatile and could lead to widespread anarchy.
item.dc:creator: Som Niroula

item.title: How Sri Lanka is Rewriting its Economic Narrative
item.description: By prioritizing transparency, the Sri Lankan government aims to rebuild trust with international lenders and investors, a critical step in stabilizing the economy.
item.dc:creator: Brian R Braun

item.title: Frontline Failure: Pakistan’s Climate Vulnerability and Leadership Gap
item.description: Despite being an extremely climate-vulnerable nation, Pakistan remains conspicuously absent from global climate leadership.
item.dc:creator: Abdul Waheed Bhutto

item.title: India’s Lagging Attempts to Contain Pollution: Bridging the Climate Commitment Gap
item.description: As India’s attempt to represent the Global South in conversations with the Global North on climate change takes center stage, there is a clear need to take action at home.
item.dc:creator: Shanthie Mariet D’Souza

item.title: The Gorge Between China and India on Hydropolitics
item.description: Miscommunication and misunderstood geography are confounding discussions about China's planned dams on the Yarlung Tsangpo gorge.
item.dc:creator: Ruth Gamble and Hongzhang Xu

item.title: Why Bangladesh Should Join ASEAN – and What Stands in the Way
item.description: Joining ASEAN is not a silver bullet for Bangladesh’s geopolitical and economic challenges, but it could be a step in the right direction.
item.dc:creator: Md Obaidullah

item.title: Putin to Visit India for First Time Since Start of Ukraine War
item.description: New Delhi will be among the few capitals to host the Russian president since the ICC issued an arrest warrant for him.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Roche

item.title: Bridging the Gap: Karzai, the Taliban, and the US Dilemma in Afghanistan
item.description: Karzai’s influence is not just historical; it lies in his ability to mediate Afghanistan’s current power struggles.
item.dc:creator: Freshta Jalalzai

item.title: India-Bangladesh-Pakistan: South Asia’s Fateful Triangle
item.description: Bangladesh’s interim government’s outreach to Pakistan is of concern to India. But Dhaka’s ties with the two countries are not a zero-sum game.
item.dc:creator: Swasti Sachdeva

item.title: Bangladesh Tilts Toward China as Its Lead Economic Partner
item.description: While Chief Adviser Muhammad Yunus’ visit to China was significant, there were no major breakthroughs.
item.dc:creator: Saqlain Rizve

item.title: The Chaos of Wage Theft in Bangladesh’s Garment Sector Requires Global Accountability
item.description: If wage theft and worker exploitation remains unresolved, Bangladesh’s development triumph will prove to be an empty miracle.
item.dc:creator: Mohammad Imon Kazi

item.title: The Perils of Ignoring the Taliban Regime’s Support for Terror Groups
item.description: While Pakistan is bearing the brunt of terrorist attacks emanating from Afghanistan, the threat is wider. Regional and global peace is in peril.
item.dc:creator: Abdul Basit

item.title: Pakistan Welcomes Starlink. But Can It Deliver on Its Promise?
item.description: Starlink could help close the urban-rural internet access gap -- if the rollout is inclusive, affordable, and thoughtfully managed.
item.dc:creator: Zohaib Alta and Nimrah Javed

item.title: Trump’s ‘Maximum Pressure’ Iran Approach Is a Headache for India
item.description: From bombing Yemen to tightening Iran sanctions, U.S. policy is testing India’s trade and connectivity strategy.
item.dc:creator: Seamus Duffy

item.title: A New Chapter in Dhaka-Beijing Ties? Why Chief Adviser Dr. Muhammad Yunus Is Visiting China
item.description: Bangladesh is looking to rebalance its diplomacy in the post-Hasina era, but it’s not going all in on China, either.
item.dc:creator: Md Obaidullah

item.title: Conflict in India’s Manipur Takes a New Turn With Intra-Community Clashes
item.description: In recent weeks, Meitei militias have clashed, as have Zomi and Hmar groups that came together against the Meitei majority.
item.dc:creator: Rajeev Bhattacharyya

item.title: India and Australia Discuss Ways to Increase Already Close Defense Cooperation
item.description: At the ninth edition of the Defense Policy Talks, they called for collaboration between their defense industries in the fields of defense science and technology.
item.dc:creator: Ajai Shukla

item.title: Bangladesh to Seal Deal with Musk’s Starlink
item.description: While officials say the decision is driven by the need to improve internet connectivity to benefit Bangladeshis, it is likely also aimed at winning Musk’s favor.
item.dc:creator: Mubashar Hasan

item.title: Will India Join the Squad?
item.description: If the grouping’s scope and agenda are restructured to focus on soft security issues, such as maritime safety, New Delhi might be more interested.
item.dc:creator: Rushali Saha

item.title: Living in Shadows: Navigating the Realities of Sex Work in Nepal
item.description: Economic necessity often pushes women into sex work. Advocates are debating the best response: decriminalization or finding alternative livelihoods.
item.dc:creator: Hritika Sharma

item.title: The Limits of Trump’s Deal-making in Afghanistan
item.description: In return for the release of an American, the U.S. dropped bounties on three top-level Taliban officials, including Sirajuddin Haqqani. But there are limits to potential Taliban-U.S. deals.
item.dc:creator: Shanthie Mariet D’Souza

item.title: India’s Energy Transition Needs Focus on Inclusion, Diversity, Equity, Accessibility
item.description: Energy transition research addressing the range of equity concerns across India’s socio-economic groups and regions is rare. This needs to change.
item.dc:creator: Aishwarya Ramachandran

item.title: New Report on Indian Muslims Recommends Nuanced Approach to Uplift Community
item.description: It calls for a “spatial approach” of targeted interventions in Muslim-concentrated spaces and “ghettos” and in sectors and occupations dominated by Muslims.
item.dc:creator: Kavita Chowdhury

item.title: What Does the Future Hold for the Development of AI in India?
item.description: It could pioneer innovative secondary uses of AI as well as alternative legal and philosophical perspectives on the technology.
item.dc:creator: Akhilesh Pillalamarri

item.title: Why the Indian Ocean, Not the Indo-Pacific, Must Anchor India’s Strategy
item.description: While New Delhi shares strategic concerns with the rest of the Quad, its Indo-Pacific approach is primarily a means to counterbalance Beijing in its immediate maritime neighborhood.
item.dc:creator: Sanchari Ghosh

item.title: Bangladesh Women Rise up Against Increasing Incidents of Rape
item.description: The Muhammad Yunus government has failed miserably in checking incidents of rape and sexual violence against women.
item.dc:creator: Samata Biswas
